title: Theory of Computation - ISRO CSE 2023  Question

questions.question:
Consider the context-free grammar \( G \) below for arithmetic expressions :
\[
\begin{array}{l}
E \rightarrow E+E|E \times E| \text { id }
\end{array}
\]

Which of the following statements is TRUE:



Theory of Computation: ISRO CSE 2023 | Question: 1
options: The string \( "\mathrm{id} \) \( +\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{id} \) " has no parse tree according to \( G \)
The string \( "\mathrm{id} \) \( +\mathrm{id} \times \) \( \mathrm{id} \)" has only one parse tree according to \( G \)
The string \( "\mathrm{id} \) \( +\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{id} \) " has exactly two parse trees according to \( G \)
The string \( "\mathrm{id} \) \( +\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{id} \) " has more than two parse trees according to \( G \)
questions.correct_answer: The string \( "\mathrm{id} \) \( +\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{id} \) " has exactly two parse trees according to \( G \)
